title: Olive Garden parent Darden's stock falls after same-store sales miss, amid weakness in flagship restaurant chains
text: Shares of Darden Restaurants Inc. DRI, -1.11% dove 2.5% in premarket trading Thursday, after the restaurant chain operator matched fiscal first-quarter profit expectations but missed on same-store sales, amid weakness in its flagship Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse chains. Net income for the quarter to Aug. 28 fell to $193.0 million, or $1.56 a share, from $230.9 million, or $1.75 a share, in the year-ago period, to match the FactSet consensus for earnings per share.
